message:
fix(vdbe/page-lock): run full observe_execution_cancellation on a 50ms cadence inside wait_for_page_lock_holder_change

Commit 5cec63b9 dropped the full `Cx::checkpoint()` out of the
per-iteration path of the page-lock wait loop because the 5ms
slice was spending 1.58% self-time there on the 2026-04-23 MT 8t
DWARF capture. The cheap fix — only probe the local
`cancel_requested` atomic — restored throughput but had a visible
downside: anything that delivers cancellation through the Cx (the
e-process oracle, native signal handlers, etc.) was no longer
observed until the *outer* loop re-entered, which for a stuck
page holder could mean waiting out the whole busy timeout before
the caller noticed a cancel it had already requested.

This commit threads the needle:

- Keep the 5ms local-atomic check as the per-slice probe; that's
  what the hot path needs for responsiveness without the
  checkpoint cost.
- Introduce `PAGE_LOCK_WAIT_FULL_CHECKPOINT_POLL = 50ms` and
  track a rolling `next_full_checkpoint` deadline, initialized
  to 50ms. Once `started.elapsed()` crosses that deadline, run
  the full `observe_execution_cancellation(cx)?` and bump the
  deadline by another 50ms (saturating, so we can't overflow on
  a genuinely long wait).
- Fold `started.elapsed()` into a single `elapsed` binding per
  iteration and reuse it for both the checkpoint-due check and
  the `remaining.saturating_sub(elapsed)` wait-budget subtract,
  so we don't call `elapsed()` twice per slice.

Result: the steady-state inner loop still executes just the
atomic probe on each 5ms slice (≈ the old post-5cec63b9 cost),
and non-local cancellation signals arrive within at most 50ms
instead of being deferred to the outer checkpoint. 50ms is
deliberately an order of magnitude larger than the 5ms slice so
the checkpoint overhead is amortized ≈10:1 against the cheap
path; on the profile that motivated 5cec63b9 this lands around
0.15% self-time — well under the original 1.58%.

The existing cancellation-responsiveness test (wait_for_page_lock
cancels within its configured slice budget) still passes because
the cheap local atomic path is unchanged; only the *full* Cx
check now has a bounded staleness ceiling. Nothing in this commit
alters the wait_budget arithmetic or the holder-change detection,
so correctness of the busy-timeout behavior is preserved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
